relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks the ordination into action.
Aaron, his sons, garments, oil, offerings, and bread are gathered.
The whole congregation gathers at the tent entrance.
Moses does as YHWH commanded.
The people are told: this is what YHWH commanded.
Aaron and sons are washed with water.
Aaron is clothed in priestly layers of service.
The breastpiece receives Urim and Thummim.
The holy crown rests on the turban.
Oil sanctifies the dwelling and all within.
Altar, vessels, basin, and stand are anointed.
Oil pours on Aaron's head.
Aaron's sons are clothed for service.
Hands rest on the purification offering.
Blood/life cleanses and sanctifies the altar.
The inward fat rises on the altar.
The rest is burned outside camp.
Hands rest on the burnt offering.
Blood/life circles the altar.
The ram is prepared and offered.
The whole offering rises as soothing scent.
Hands rest on the ram of filling.
Aaron's ear, thumb, and toe are marked for holy hearing, doing, and walking.
The sons receive the same marks of consecrated service.
The rich portions and right thigh are taken.
Bread joins the portions for the wave offering.
The offering fills the palms of Aaron and sons.
The filled-hand offering rises on the altar.
Moses receives the waved breast as his portion.
Oil and blood/life sanctify people and garments.
Aaron and sons eat at the tent entrance.
The remaining flesh and bread are burned.
Seven days complete the filling of their hands.
Atonement/covering is made as YHWH commanded.
They keep watch at the tent entrance day and night.
Aaron and his sons do all commanded through Moses.

contemplative_note: Leviticus 8 turns priesthood into embodied accountability: washed, clothed, anointed, marked at ear, hand, and foot, and kept seven days at the threshold of Presence.
